title: Authors and Citation
text: Coverdale C (2026). ukhousing: Access UK Housing Data Land Registry, EPC, Planning. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/charlescoverdale/ukhousing.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ukhousing: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning},   author = {Charles Coverdale},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/charlescoverdale/ukhousing}, }

what: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: Access UK housing data official sources R. House prices, individual property transactions, energy performance certificates, planning data, rental price indices.

what: The UK has world-class housing data. It is also a mess.
title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: UK publishes richest housing data anywhere world. Every residential transaction England Wales since 1995 public. Every Energy Performance Certificate since 2008 queryable postcode. Planning applications, brownfield land registers, affordability ratios, rental price indices, stamp duty receipts available cost, long back-series, local authority resolution. catch data scattered across roughly ten institutions, format, access pattern, quirks: HM Land Registry publishes UK House Price Index via linked-data SPARQL endpoint plus REST API, Price Paid Data multi-gigabyte bulk CSVs header row MHCLG’s EPC Open Data service requires free registered API key HTTP Basic Auth, paginates using search-tokens, 90+ hyphenated fields per record planning.data.gov.uk (Digital Land) clean REST API JSON GeoJSON output ONS publishes rental price indices via Beta API, affordability ratios Excel workbooks multi-row headers DLUHC publishes housing supply data ODS live tables hash-based URLs change every republication HMRC publishes Stamp Duty Land Tax statistics ODS/XLSX VOA publishes private rental market data separately, also ODS Registers Scotland NI Land & Property Services hold equivalent data jurisdictions different formats ukhousing wraps four stable API-based sources (Land Registry HPI, Land Registry Price Paid, EPC, planning.data.gov.uk) plus ONS PIPR rental prices, exposes consistent R interface. flaky Excel/ODS scraping sources deliberately scope: change format often maintain reliably.

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
code: install.packages("ukhousing")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/ukhousing")

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: sources free. EPC requires free registration API key. SPARQL escape hatch (ukh_sparql()) provided queries dedicated helpers don’t cover.

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
code: library(ukhousing)  # UK average house prices, monthly uk <- ukh_hpi("united-kingdom", from = "2020-01-01") head(uk) #>         date           region   hpi avg_price pct_change_monthly pct_change_annual #> 1 2020-01-01   united-kingdom 125.3    232000                0.3              2.1 #> 2 2020-02-01   united-kingdom 125.8    232900                0.4              2.3 #> ...  # All Westminster flats sold in 2024 wm <- ukh_ppd(2024, la = "Westminster", property_type = "flat") head(wm)  # Energy rating distribution for Westminster ukh_epc_set_key("you@example.com", "your_api_key") ukh_epc_summary(la = "E09000033")

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
code: # Compare prices across regions prices <- ukh_hpi_compare(   c("london", "manchester", "newcastle-upon-tyne"),   measure = "avg_price",   from = "2015-01-01" ) plot(prices$date, prices$london, type = "l", ylim = range(prices[-1], na.rm = TRUE)) lines(prices$date, prices$manchester, col = "red") lines(prices$date, prices$`newcastle-upon-tyne`, col = "blue")

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: Land Registry publishes every residential property sale England Wales since 1995. yearly file 150 MB contains ~900,000 transactions.
code: # Detached houses sold in Manchester for over GBP 1m during 2024 manchester <- ukh_ppd(   year = 2024,   la = "Manchester",   property_type = "detached" ) manchester[manchester$price > 1e6, c("date", "postcode", "price", "street")]  # Monthly transaction counts nationally summary <- ukh_ppd_summary(2024, by = "month") plot(as.Date(paste0(summary$month, "-01")), summary$n, type = "l")

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
code: # Search EPCs epcs <- ukh_epc_search(postcode = "SW1A 1AA") table(epcs$current_energy_rating)  # Summary for a whole local authority ukh_epc_summary(la = "E09000033") #>   rating count percentage mean_floor_area mean_co2_emissions #> 1      A    12        0.1            85.3               2.1 #> 2      B  1523       13.2            82.6               3.8 #> ...

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
code: # Brownfield land register bf <- ukh_planning("brownfield-land", limit = 500)  # All available datasets datasets <- ukh_planning_datasets()

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: EPC functions accept type = c("domestic", "non-domestic", "display").

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: Downloads cached tools::R_user_dir("ukhousing", "cache") default. Subsequent calls instant. Override location options(ukhousing.cache_dir = "/path//dir"). Use ukh_clear_cache() remove cached files.

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: Energy Performance Certificate service requires free API key. Register https://epc.opendatacommunities.org/ :
code: ukh_epc_set_key("you@example.com", "your_api_key")  # Or set env vars in .Renviron: # EPC_EMAIL=you@example.com # EPC_API_KEY=your_api_key

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: UK HPI: 441+ areas, January 1995 (E&W), 2004 (Scotland), 2005 (Northern Ireland) Price Paid Data: every residential sale England Wales since 1995 (~28 million records) EPC: ~25 million domestic certificates, 2008 onwards (England Wales) Planning: 100+ datasets covering full England planning system

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: Report bugs request features GitHub Issues.

title: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning
text: UK housing, land registry, price paid data, UK HPI, EPC, energy performance certificate, planning, brownfield land, MHCLG, housing data, house prices, property, real estate

what: Inspect the local ukhousing cache — ukh_cache_info
title: Inspect the local ukhousing cache — ukh_cache_info
text: Returns information local cache: lives, many files contains, much disk space take. Useful debugging stale results deciding whether call ukh_clear_cache().

title: Inspect the local ukhousing cache — ukh_cache_info
code: ukh_cache_info()

title: Inspect the local ukhousing cache — ukh_cache_info
text: list elements dir, n_files, size_bytes, size_human, files (data frame).

title: Inspect the local ukhousing cache — ukh_cache_info
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) ukh_cache_info() #> $dir #> [1] "/tmp/RtmpbOUQAH" #>  #> $n_files #> [1] 2 #>  #> $size_bytes #> [1] 8192 #>  #> $size_human #> [1] "8.0 KB" #>  #> $files #>                                     name size_bytes            modified #> 1 bslib-246362e7e3ff6191071d5f9b40ba8d62       4096 2026-04-24 08:18:11 #> 2                                downlit       4096 2026-04-24 08:18:13 #>  options(op) # }

title: Clear the ukhousing cache — ukh_clear_cache
text: Deletes locally cached UK housing data files. next call data function re-download fresh data.

title: Clear the ukhousing cache — ukh_clear_cache
code: ukh_clear_cache()

title: Clear the ukhousing cache — ukh_clear_cache
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Clear the ukhousing cache — ukh_clear_cache
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) ukh_clear_cache() #> Removed 2 cached files from /tmp/RtmpbOUQAH. options(op) # }

what: Download a bulk EPC ZIP for a local authority — ukh_epc_bulk
title: Download a bulk EPC ZIP for a local authority — ukh_epc_bulk
text: Downloads extracts MHCLG bulk ZIP containing domestic certificates recommendations one local authority. ZIP typically 5 50 MB contains certificates.csv, recommendations.csv, licence file.

title: Download a bulk EPC ZIP for a local authority — ukh_epc_bulk
code: ukh_epc_bulk(   la,   refresh = FALSE,   type = c("domestic", "non-domestic", "display") )

title: Download a bulk EPC ZIP for a local authority — ukh_epc_bulk
text: la Character. Local authority GSS code (e.g. "E09000033"). refresh Logical. Re-download even cached? Default FALSE. type Character. Register: "domestic" (default), "non-domestic", "display".

title: Download a bulk EPC ZIP for a local authority — ukh_epc_bulk
text: list elements certificates recommendations, giving path extracted CSV.

title: Download a bulk EPC ZIP for a local authority — ukh_epc_bulk
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ paths <- ukh_epc_bulk("E09000033") certs <- read.csv(paths$certificates) } # }

what: Fetch a single EPC by LMK key — ukh_epc_certificate
title: Fetch a single EPC by LMK key — ukh_epc_certificate
text: Returns available fields improvement recommendations one Energy Performance Certificate identified LMK key.

title: Fetch a single EPC by LMK key — ukh_epc_certificate
code: ukh_epc_certificate(lmk_key, type = c("domestic", "non-domestic", "display"))

title: Fetch a single EPC by LMK key — ukh_epc_certificate
text: lmk_key Character. certificate's LMK key (found lmk_key column ukh_epc_search() results). type Character. Register: "domestic" (default), "non-domestic", "display".

title: Fetch a single EPC by LMK key — ukh_epc_certificate
text: list two elements: certificate one-row data frame certificate fields. recommendations data frame improvement recommendations (may empty).

title: Fetch a single EPC by LMK key — ukh_epc_certificate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cert <- ukh_epc_certificate("0000-0000-0000-0000-0000") cert$certificate cert$recommendations } # }

what: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
title: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
text: Aggregates improvement recommendations across certificates local authority, returning frequency recommendation mean estimated cost savings available.

title: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
code: ukh_epc_recommendations_summary(   la,   type = c("domestic", "non-domestic", "display"),   refresh = FALSE )

title: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
text: la Character. Local authority GSS code. type Character. "domestic" (default), "non-domestic", "display". refresh Logical. Re-download bulk ZIP? Default FALSE.

title: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
text: data frame columns improvement_id, improvement_summary, count, mean_indicative_cost (numeric costs reported), ordered count descending.

title: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
text: uses bulk per-LA ZIP download rather paginated API, much faster aggregation. Requires EPC API credentials.

title: Summarise EPC improvement recommendations for a local authority — ukh_epc_recommendations_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ recs <- ukh_epc_recommendations_summary("E09000033") head(recs) } # }

what: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
title: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
text: Queries MHCLG EPC Open Data service domestic certificates matching given filters. Results paginated automatically using search-tokens (parameter, caps 10,000 records).

title: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
code: ukh_epc_search(   postcode = NULL,   la = NULL,   property_type = NULL,   rating = NULL,   built_form = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   size = 1000L,   max_records = 10000L,   type = c("domestic", "non-domestic", "display") )

title: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
text: postcode Optional character. Postcode partial postcode. la Optional character. Local authority GSS code (e.g. "E09000033" Westminster). property_type Optional character. "House", "Flat", "Bungalow", "Maisonette", "Park home". rating Optional character. Current energy rating ("" "G"). built_form Optional character. "Detached", "Semi-Detached", "End-Terrace", "Mid-Terrace", "Enclosed End-Terrace", "Enclosed Mid-Terrace". , Optional. Lodgement date range (YYYY-MM-DD). size Integer. Results per page, max 5000. Default 1000. max_records Integer. Maximum total records fetch across pages. Default 10000. Set higher bulk analysis. type Character. Register query: "domestic" (default, housing), "non-domestic" (commercial buildings), "display" (DECs public buildings).

title: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
text: data frame certificates. Columns include lmk_key, address, postcode, uprn, local_authority, constituency, property_type, built_form, inspection_date, lodgement_date, current_energy_rating, current_energy_efficiency, potential_energy_rating, potential_energy_efficiency, total_floor_area, co2_emissions_current, .

title: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
text: Registration https://epc.opendatacommunities.org/ required free.

title: Search domestic Energy Performance Certificates — ukh_epc_search
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Requires EPC API credentials certs <- ukh_epc_search(postcode = "SW1A 1AA") head(certs)  # All E-rated flats in Westminster lodged since 2020 wm <- ukh_epc_search(   la = "E09000033", property_type = "Flat",   rating = "E", from = "2020-01-01" ) } # }

title: Set EPC API credentials — ukh_epc_set_key
text: Stores email API key used authenticate requests MHCLG Energy Performance Certificate Open Data service. Credentials persist current R session. Alternatively, set EPC_EMAIL EPC_API_KEY environment variables .Renviron file.

title: Set EPC API credentials — ukh_epc_set_key
code: ukh_epc_set_key(email, key)

title: Set EPC API credentials — ukh_epc_set_key
text: email Character. email registered . key Character. API key.

title: Set EPC API credentials — ukh_epc_set_key
text: Invisible NULL.

title: Set EPC API credentials — ukh_epc_set_key
text: Register free API key https://epc.opendatacommunities.org/.

title: Set EPC API credentials — ukh_epc_set_key
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ukh_epc_set_key("you@example.com", "your_api_key_here") } # }

what: EPC rating distribution for a local authority — ukh_epc_summary
title: EPC rating distribution for a local authority — ukh_epc_summary
text: Summarises distribution current energy ratings (-G) domestic certificates local authority. Useful area-level comparisons housing stock efficiency.

title: EPC rating distribution for a local authority — ukh_epc_summary
code: ukh_epc_summary(   la,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   type = c("domestic", "non-domestic", "display") )

title: EPC rating distribution for a local authority — ukh_epc_summary
text: la Character. Local authority GSS code. , Optional. Lodgement date range. type Character. Register: "domestic" (default), "non-domestic", "display".

title: EPC rating distribution for a local authority — ukh_epc_summary
text: data frame one row per rating (-G) columns rating, count, percentage, mean_floor_area, mean_co2_emissions.

title: EPC rating distribution for a local authority — ukh_epc_summary
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ukh_epc_summary(la = "E09000033") } # }

title: UK House Price Index — ukh_hpi
text: Fetches monthly UK House Price Index data region HM Land Registry linked data service. Coverage: 441+ areas (countries, regions, counties, local authorities) January 1995 (England Wales), January 2004 (Scotland), January 2005 (Northern Ireland).

title: UK House Price Index — ukh_hpi
code: ukh_hpi(region, from = NULL, to = NULL, refresh = FALSE)

title: UK House Price Index — ukh_hpi
text: region Character. Region slug, GSS code, common name (e.g. "westminster", "Westminster", "E09000033"). See ukh_regions() common options. , Optional character Date. Start end dates ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD). Default returns full available history. refresh Logical. Re-download even cached? Default FALSE.

title: UK House Price Index — ukh_hpi
text: data frame one row per month. Columns include date, region, hpi, avg_price, pct_change_monthly, pct_change_annual, sales_volume, plus property-type buyer-type average prices.

title: UK House Price Index — ukh_hpi
text: returned data frame includes headline index, average price, monthly annual percentage change, sales volume, breakdowns property type (detached, semi-detached, terraced, flat) buyer type (cash, mortgage, first-time buyer, former owner occupier, new build, existing property). Sales volumes lag headline index approximately five months Land Registry needs full transaction set settle.

title: UK House Price Index — ukh_hpi

what: Compare UK HPI across multiple regions — ukh_hpi_compare
title: Compare UK HPI across multiple regions — ukh_hpi_compare
text: Fetches UK House Price Index several regions returns wide data frame one measure (e.g. average price annual % change) column per region. Useful regional comparison charts.

title: Compare UK HPI across multiple regions — ukh_hpi_compare
code: ukh_hpi_compare(   regions,   measure = "avg_price",   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   refresh = FALSE )

title: Compare UK HPI across multiple regions — ukh_hpi_compare
text: regions Character vector region slugs, GSS codes, names. measure Character. measure return. One "avg_price", "hpi", "pct_change_annual", "pct_change_monthly", "sales_volume". Default "avg_price". , Optional character Date. Date range. refresh Logical. Re-download even cached? Default FALSE.

title: Compare UK HPI across multiple regions — ukh_hpi_compare
text: wide data frame date column one column per region.

title: Compare UK HPI across multiple regions — ukh_hpi_compare
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir())  prices <- ukh_hpi_compare(   c("london", "manchester", "newcastle-upon-tyne"),   measure = "avg_price",   from = "2015-01-01" ) #> ℹ Downloading UK HPI for "london"... #> ℹ Downloading UK HPI for "manchester"... #> ℹ Downloading UK HPI for "newcastle-upon-tyne"... head(prices) #>         date london manchester newcastle-upon-tyne #> 1 2015-01-01 431270         NA                  NA #> 2 2015-02-01 433331         NA                  NA #> 3 2015-03-01 433259         NA                  NA #> 4 2015-04-01 439403         NA                  NA #> 5 2015-05-01 445154         NA                  NA #> 6 2015-06-01 449070         NA                  NA  options(op) # }

what: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
title: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
text: Fetches UK's official private-rental price index ONS Beta API. dataset replaced Index Private Housing Rental Prices (IPHRP) Price Index Private Rents (PIPR) January 2024, dataset slug covers .

title: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
code: ukh_pipr(refresh = FALSE)

title: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
text: refresh Logical. Re-download even cached? Default FALSE.

title: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
text: data frame columns date, region, index.

title: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
text: Coverage: UK aggregate plus countries (England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland) English regions, monthly January 2015.

title: ONS Index of Private Housing Rental Prices / Price Index of Private Rents — ukh_pipr
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) rents <- ukh_pipr() #> ℹ Downloading ONS Price Index of Private Rents... #> Warning: No observations returned from ONS PIPR. head(rents) #> [1] date   region index  #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) options(op) # }

title: Query planning.data.gov.uk — ukh_planning
text: Fetches records Digital Land planning data platform, hosts 100 datasets planning applications, brownfield land, local plans, conservation areas, listed buildings, flood risk zones, .

title: Query planning.data.gov.uk — ukh_planning
code: ukh_planning(   dataset,   la = NULL,   limit = 1000L,   format = c("data.frame", "raw", "sf") )

title: Query planning.data.gov.uk — ukh_planning
text: dataset Character. Dataset slug. Common values: "planning-application", "brownfield-land", "local-plan", "conservation-area", "listed-building", "article-4-direction-area", "tree-preservation-zone", "flood-risk-zone". See ukh_planning_datasets() full list. la Optional character. Local authority name (matched case-insensitively organisation field). limit Integer. Maximum records return. Default 1000. format Character. Response format. "data.frame" (default) returns tidy data. "raw" returns parsed JSON list.

title: Query planning.data.gov.uk — ukh_planning
text: data frame (list format = "raw").

title: Query planning.data.gov.uk — ukh_planning
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) bf <- ukh_planning("brownfield-land", limit = 100) head(bf) #>   entry.date start.date   end.date  entity   name         dataset  typology #> 1 2024-12-16 2017-11-01            1700000 BFR001 brownfield-land geography #> 2 2024-12-16 2017-11-01            1700001 BFR002 brownfield-land geography #> 3 2024-12-16 2017-11-01 title: Price Paid Data — ukh_ppd
text: Fetches individual property transaction records HM Land Registry Price Paid Data, filtered local authority, postcode, property type, criteria.

title: Price Paid Data — ukh_ppd
code: ukh_ppd(   year = as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")),   la = NULL,   postcode = NULL,   property_type = NULL,   new_build = NULL,   tenure = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   refresh = FALSE )

title: Price Paid Data — ukh_ppd
text: year Integer. Year transactions fetch. Defaults current calendar year. la Optional character. Local authority name (matched case-insensitively district field). postcode Optional character. Postcode postcode prefix. property_type Optional character. One "detached", "semi", "terraced", "flat", "". Also accepts single-letter codes "D", "S", "T", "F", "O". new_build Optional logical. TRUE, newly built properties. FALSE, established properties. tenure Optional character. "freehold" "leasehold". , Optional character Date. Date range within year (YYYY-MM-DD). refresh Logical. Re-download yearly file even cached? Default FALSE.

title: Price Paid Data — ukh_ppd
text: data frame individual transactions columns transaction_id, price, date, postcode, property_type, new_build, tenure, paon, saon, street, locality, town, district, county, category, record_status.

title: Price Paid Data — ukh_ppd
text: full yearly CSV ~150 MB (900,000 transactions). function downloads yearly file, caches , filters memory. Memory footprint call roughly 1 GB R data frames considerably larger source CSV; memory-constrained machines, prefer ukh_ppd_address() postcode lookups ukh_ppd_summary() aggregated stats. Subsequent queries year use cache. multi-year queries, call function per year use ukh_ppd_years().

title: Price Paid Data — ukh_ppd
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir())  # Westminster flats sold in 2024 wm <- ukh_ppd(2024, la = "Westminster", property_type = "flat") #> ℹ Downloading pp-2024.csv from HM Land Registry... head(wm) #>  [1] transaction_id price          date           postcode       property_type  #>  [6] new_build      tenure         paon           saon           street         #> [11] locality       town           district       county         category       #> [16] record_status  #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)  options(op) # }

what: Look up Price Paid transactions by postcode address — ukh_ppd_address
title: Look up Price Paid transactions by postcode address — ukh_ppd_address
text: Uses Land Registry linked data address lookup find transactions given postcode. Faster downloading yearly bulk file want single postcode.

title: Look up Price Paid transactions by postcode address — ukh_ppd_address
code: ukh_ppd_address(postcode)

title: Look up Price Paid transactions by postcode address — ukh_ppd_address
text: postcode Character. Full UK postcode (e.g. "SW1A 1AA").

title: Look up Price Paid transactions by postcode address — ukh_ppd_address
text: data frame transactions addresses matching postcode.

title: Look up Price Paid transactions by postcode address — ukh_ppd_address
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) tx <- ukh_ppd_address("SW1A 1AA") head(tx) #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows options(op) # }

what: Download a Price Paid Data bulk file — ukh_ppd_bulk
title: Download a Price Paid Data bulk file — ukh_ppd_bulk
text: Downloads caches yearly (complete) Price Paid Data CSV HM Land Registry. Returns path cached file. Yearly files typically 100-200 MB; complete file (1995-present) approximately 5.3 GB.

title: Download a Price Paid Data bulk file — ukh_ppd_bulk
code: ukh_ppd_bulk(   year = as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")),   full = FALSE,   refresh = FALSE )

title: Download a Price Paid Data bulk file — ukh_ppd_bulk
text: year Integer. Year download. Ignored full = TRUE. Defaults current year. full Logical. TRUE, download complete file (pp-complete.csv, ~5.3 GB) instead single year. Default FALSE. refresh Logical. Re-download even cached? Default FALSE.

title: Download a Price Paid Data bulk file — ukh_ppd_bulk
text: Character. path cached CSV file.

title: Download a Price Paid Data bulk file — ukh_ppd_bulk
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) path <- ukh_ppd_bulk(2025) #> ℹ Downloading pp-2025.csv from HM Land Registry... options(op) # }

title: Price Paid Data summary — ukh_ppd_summary
text: Returns summary statistics Price Paid Data given year, aggregated month, property type, local authority. Useful user wants counts median prices without loading 150 MB individual transactions.

title: Price Paid Data summary — ukh_ppd_summary
code: ukh_ppd_summary(   year = as.integer(format(Sys.Date(), "%Y")),   by = c("month", "property_type", "la"),   la = NULL,   refresh = FALSE )

title: Price Paid Data summary — ukh_ppd_summary
text: year Integer. Year summarise. Character. Aggregation dimension: "month", "property_type", "la". Default "month". la Optional character. Restrict one local authority. refresh Logical. Re-download even cached? Default FALSE.

title: Price Paid Data summary — ukh_ppd_summary
text: data frame one row per group columns n, median_price, mean_price, total_value.

title: Price Paid Data summary — ukh_ppd_summary

what: Look up a single Price Paid transaction by ID — ukh_ppd_transaction
title: Look up a single Price Paid transaction by ID — ukh_ppd_transaction
text: Fetches full metadata one transaction Land Registry linked data service transaction unique identifier.

title: Look up a single Price Paid transaction by ID — ukh_ppd_transaction
code: ukh_ppd_transaction(id)

title: Look up a single Price Paid transaction by ID — ukh_ppd_transaction
text: id Character. transaction unique identifier (GUID, without curly braces).

title: Look up a single Price Paid transaction by ID — ukh_ppd_transaction
text: one-row data frame transaction fields, empty data frame transaction found.

title: Look up a single Price Paid transaction by ID — ukh_ppd_transaction
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) tx <- ukh_ppd_transaction("{A4C5B0C6-4D5D-47E2-E053-6C04A8C07E7C}") tx #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows options(op) # }

what: Price Paid Data across multiple years — ukh_ppd_years
title: Price Paid Data across multiple years — ukh_ppd_years
text: Convenience wrapper calls ukh_ppd() year vector row-binds results. Caches year independently.

title: Price Paid Data across multiple years — ukh_ppd_years
code: ukh_ppd_years(years, ...)

title: Price Paid Data across multiple years — ukh_ppd_years
text: years Integer vector. Years fetch. ... Additional arguments passed ukh_ppd() (e.g. la, postcode, property_type).

title: Price Paid Data across multiple years — ukh_ppd_years
text: single data frame combining transactions requested years.

title: Price Paid Data across multiple years — ukh_ppd_years
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) five_year <- ukh_ppd_years(2020:2024, la = "Westminster",                            property_type = "flat") #> ℹ Downloading pp-2020.csv from HM Land Registry... #> ℹ Downloading pp-2021.csv from HM Land Registry... #> ℹ Downloading pp-2022.csv from HM Land Registry... #> ℹ Downloading pp-2023.csv from HM Land Registry... #> ℹ Loading pp-2024.csv from cache. nrow(five_year) #> [1] 0 options(op) # }

what: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
title: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
text: Returns data frame common UK HPI region slugs names, GSS codes, tier (country, region, county, local authority). Useful looking slug pass ukh_hpi().

title: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
code: ukh_regions(tier = c("all", "country", "region", "county", "la"))

title: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
text: tier Character. Filter tier: "" (default), "country", "region", "county", "la".

title: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
text: data frame columns slug, name, gss_code, tier.

title: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
text: selection commonly used regions, exhaustive list. UK HPI covers 441+ areas total; valid slug can passed ukh_hpi() directly.

title: List valid UK HPI region slugs — ukh_regions
code: # All regions head(ukh_regions()) #>               slug             name  gss_code    tier #> 1   united-kingdom   United Kingdom K02000001 country #> 2          england          England E92000001 country #> 3            wales            Wales W92000004 country #> 4         scotland         Scotland S92000003 country #> 5 northern-ireland Northern Ireland N92000002 country #> 6    great-britain    Great Britain K03000001 country  # Just the nine English regions ukh_regions(tier = "region") #>                       slug                     name  gss_code   tier #> 1               north-east               North East E12000001 region #> 2               north-west               North West E12000002 region #> 3 yorkshire-and-the-humber Yorkshire and The Humber E12000003 region #> 4            east-midlands            East Midlands E12000004 region #> 5            west-midlands            West Midlands E12000005 region #> 6          east-of-england          East of England E12000006 region #> 7                   london                   London E12000007 region #> 8               south-east               South East E12000008 region #> 9               south-west               South West E12000009 region  # Country-level series ukh_regions(tier = "country") #>               slug             name  gss_code    tier #> 1   united-kingdom   United Kingdom K02000001 country #> 2          england          England E92000001 country #> 3            wales            Wales W92000004 country #> 4         scotland         Scotland S92000003 country #> 5 northern-ireland Northern Ireland N92000002 country #> 6    great-britain    Great Britain K03000001 country

title: Run a SPARQL query — ukh_sparql
text: Runs SPARQL query one supported endpoints returns result data frame. Useful queries covered dedicated helpers, including custom HPI aggregations Price Paid Data lookups postcode.

title: Run a SPARQL query — ukh_sparql
code: ukh_sparql(   query,   endpoint = c("land-registry", "opendatacommunities"),   timeout = 60L )

title: Run a SPARQL query — ukh_sparql
text: query Character. SPARQL query string. endpoint Character. One "land-registry" (default), "opendatacommunities", full URL SPARQL endpoint. timeout Integer. Request timeout seconds. Default 60.

title: Run a SPARQL query — ukh_sparql
text: data frame bindings. Column names match SELECT variables query.

title: Run a SPARQL query — ukh_sparql
text: endpoints free require authentication. Land Registry endpoint covers HPI Price Paid Data; Open Data Communities endpoint covers 300+ housing-market datasets published MHCLG.

title: Run a SPARQL query — ukh_sparql
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir())  # All HPI observations for Westminster in January 2020 q <- ' PREFIX ukhpi: <http://landregistry.data.gov.uk/def/ukhpi/> SELECT ?hpi ?avgPrice WHERE {   <http://landregistry.data.gov.uk/data/ukhpi/region/city-of-westminster/month/2020-01>     ukhpi:housePriceIndex ?hpi ;     ukhpi:averagePrice    ?avgPrice . }' ukh_sparql(q) #>     hpi avgPrice #> 1 87.50  1072220  options(op) # }

what: Transaction volumes for a region — ukh_transactions
title: Transaction volumes for a region — ukh_transactions
text: Returns monthly residential transaction counts UK region. thin wrapper ukh_hpi() extracts sales_volume column date. Transaction volumes lag headline index approximately five months Land Registry needs full transaction set settle.

title: Transaction volumes for a region — ukh_transactions
code: ukh_transactions(region, from = NULL, to = NULL, refresh = FALSE)

title: Transaction volumes for a region — ukh_transactions
text: region Character. Region slug, GSS code, common name. , Optional. Date range (YYYY-MM-DD). refresh Logical. Re-download? Default FALSE.

title: Transaction volumes for a region — ukh_transactions
text: data frame date, region, sales_volume.

title: Transaction volumes for a region — ukh_transactions
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(ukhousing.cache_dir = tempdir()) tx <- ukh_transactions("england", from = "2020-01-01") #> ℹ Downloading UK HPI for "england"... head(tx) #>         date  region sales_volume #> 1 2020-01-01 england        56603 #> 2 2020-02-01 england        56568 #> 3 2020-03-01 england        57626 #> 4 2020-04-01 england        23673 #> 5 2020-05-01 england        30798 #> 6 2020-06-01 england        46664 options(op) # }

what: ukhousing: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning — ukhousing-package
title: ukhousing: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning — ukhousing-package
text: Fetch UK housing data official sources. Access UK House Price Index Price Paid Data 'HM Land Registry' https://landregistry.data.gov.uk/, domestic non-domestic Energy Performance Certificates 'MHCLG' Open Data service https://epc.opendatacommunities.org/, planning application, brownfield land, local plan data 'planning.data.gov.uk' https://www.planning.data.gov.uk/. Data covers 441 UK local authorities 1995 present. Functions accept flexible filters (postcode, local authority, property type, date range) return tidy data frames. Downloaded data cached locally subsequent calls.

title: ukhousing: Access UK Housing Data from Land Registry, EPC, and Planning — ukhousing-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-21 Initial CRAN release.

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: ukh_hpi() fetches UK House Price Index data 441+ regions (countries, English regions, counties, local authorities) 1995 onwards. Includes average prices, index values, percentage changes, sales volumes, breakdowns property type buyer type. ukh_hpi_compare() fetches one measure across multiple regions wide data frame. ukh_transactions() returns monthly transaction volumes region (extracts sales_volume series ukh_hpi(), noting ~5-month lag). ukh_ppd() fetches individual property transactions Price Paid Data, filtered local authority, postcode, property type, tenure, new-build status, date range. ukh_ppd_bulk() downloads yearly complete Price Paid CSVs, automatic fallback split part files larger years. ukh_ppd_summary() returns aggregated statistics (counts, median mean prices) month, property type, local authority. ukh_ppd_years() fetches PPD across multiple years one call row-binds. ukh_ppd_transaction() looks single transaction GUID via Land Registry linked-data REST API. ukh_ppd_address() looks transactions postcode via address lookup endpoint, avoiding full yearly download.

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: ukh_epc_set_key() stores EPC API credentials session. ukh_epc_search() queries certificates filters (postcode, local authority, property type, energy rating, built form, date range). Pagination handled automatically via search-tokens. Supports type = "domestic" (default), "non-domestic", "display". ukh_epc_certificate() fetches single certificate 90+ fields plus improvement recommendations. ukh_epc_summary() returns distribution energy ratings (-G) local authority. ukh_epc_bulk() downloads extracts per-LA bulk ZIP. ukh_epc_recommendations_summary() aggregates improvement recommendations across local authority.

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: ukh_planning() queries planning.data.gov.uk brownfield land, planning applications, local plans, conservation areas, listed buildings, . Supports format = "sf" return simple features GeoJSON-capable datasets (requires sf package, declared Suggests). ukh_planning_datasets() lists 100+ available datasets.

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: ukh_pipr() fetches ONS Price Index Private Rents (monthly, UK regional, January 2015) via ONS Beta API.

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: ukh_sparql() runs arbitrary SPARQL query Land Registry endpoint (HPI + PPD) Open Data Communities endpoint (300+ MHCLG housing-market datasets).

title: ukhousing 0.1.0
text: ukh_regions() returns lookup table common UK HPI region slugs names, GSS codes, tier. ukh_clear_cache() empties local download cache. ukh_cache_info() reports cached much space uses.
